methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Atoms is a Series D+ stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $1.55B+ to date. The valuation analysis operates within a broader robotics and manufacturing sector comprising one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median triangulation method is not applicable to this analysis. The robotics and manufacturing sector contains 11 same-sector peers overall, but filtering by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints at any meaningful narrowing. A median derived from all-sector peers would conflate vastly different business models and capital requirements and therefore cannot reliably inform valuation. The insufficient depth of comparables at Atoms' stage and valuation tier prevents reliable quantitative anchoring through this method.

Without applicable triangulation approaches supported by adequate comparable data, this analysis cannot establish a defensible valuation range. Completion of a rigorous valuation would require either expanding the dataset through additional private market transactions, applying alternative methods (DCF, sum-of-the-parts, or precedent transaction analysis if relevant M&A data surfaces), or identifying additional robotics peers at similar stage and scale. The primary constraint is the sparsity of publicly disclosed late-stage private robotics financings that match Atoms' profile.
